children.name: Payload size: API has reasonable size limits
Payload size: server rejects oversized payloads
Rate limiting: excessive requests are throttled
Rate limiting: 429 status includes Retry-After header
Rate limiting (OWASP API4): DoS protection on public endpoints
Rate limiting: per-IP or per-user limits exist
Auth cookie flags from login response (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite)
Broken authentication: weak passwords are rejected
Broken authentication: no default credentials accepted
Broken authentication: password reset requires verification
Broken authentication: enumeration protection on login
Authentication bypass: direct dashboard access without credentials
XSS prevention: user data sanitization in dashboard
Brute-force: repeated bad logins are throttled/locked
JWT tokens properly validated against tampering and algorithm confusion
UI logout clears browser session state
Session ID rotates on login to prevent fixation attacks
Idle sessions are properly terminated after inactivity
Expired sessions return 401 from API endpoints
Session timeout: UI logout should invalidate browser session token
UI login shows generic error messages to prevent user enumeration
UI login displays rate limiting feedback to users
UI Login: password field masked by default
XSS/Storage: sensitive tokens not in localStorage
XSS: sessionStorage does not contain sensitive data
API should return 4xx for invalid fields, not 500 with stack traces
Mass assignment: updating card limits should not permit unauthorized fields
Excessive Data Exposure: GET user should not return sensitive fields (password)
Excessive Data Exposure: API should not expose tokens or secrets
IDOR: users cannot access other users resources by ID
Mass assignment: creating user should not allow isAdmin=true
Mass assignment: creating user should not allow role assignment
Mass assignment: creating virtual card should not allow sensitive fields
Audit: zero critical/high risk issues for release
Audit: complete OWASP API Top 10 coverage
Regression: sensitive data not exposed in responses
CORS: credentials not allowed with wildcard origin
CORS: dangerous methods blocked without proper origin
CSRF: browser token rotates after login
CSRF: browser token invalidated after logout
CSRF: browser token rotates after a sensitive action
CSRF: requests without token are rejected
CSRF: double submit cookie pattern or synchronizer token
CSRF: token not leaked in URL
Boundary Values: numeric edge cases handled correctly
Boundary Values: string length limits enforced
Boundary Values: null and undefined handled safely
Boundary Values: array size limits enforced
Malformed JSON: user creation endpoint handles invalid JSON
Malformed JSON: deeply nested structures handled safely
Malformed JSON: large payload limits enforced
Fuzzing: user creation endpoint handles random inputs
Fuzzing: authentication endpoint handles random inputs
Fuzzing: search/filter endpoints handle random inputs
Clickjacking: page cannot be embedded in iframe from different origin
HSTS: Strict-Transport-Security header present on HTTPS
Security headers: X-XSS-Protection removed or set to 0
Security headers: Cache-Control for sensitive pages
Security headers: no cache for authenticated resources
Security Headers (OWASP API7): comprehensive security and fingerprinting check
File upload: malicious file extensions blocked
File upload: file size limits enforced
File upload: uploaded files not executable
SQLi: SQL injection in login form
SQLi/NoSQLi: input sanitization in API endpoints
Injection (OWASP API8): SQL/command injection probe should not return server error
XSS: reflected XSS via URL parameters
XSS: stored XSS in user input fields
XSS: inline script execution is blocked by CSP
XSS: output encoding for user-generated content
Escape Chars: SQL injection characters properly escaped
Escape Chars: XSS characters properly encoded
Escape Chars: command injection metacharacters filtered
Escape Chars: path traversal sequences blocked
File Traversal: basic directory traversal blocked
File Traversal: encoded traversal attempts blocked
File Traversal: null byte injection prevented
Role scoping: regular user cannot access admin endpoints
Role scoping: role elevation attempts blocked
Role scoping: JWT does not expose sensitive role claims
Role scoping: operations limited by scope
SRI: external scripts have integrity attributes
SRI: external stylesheets have integrity attributes
SRI: integrity hashes use strong algorithms
SRI: crossorigin attribute present with integrity
Supply chain: CDN resources use HTTPS
Supply chain: no untrusted CDN sources
Dependencies: package.json exists and has dependencies
Dependencies: package-lock.json exists for reproducible builds
Dependencies: no known vulnerable packages (check advisories)
Dependencies: version pinning or ranges are safe
Supply chain: .npmrc or similar config exists
Supply chain: no credentials in package files
Third-party scripts: analytics scripts use async/defer
Third-party scripts: limited to necessary domains
Third-party scripts: CSP allows only trusted domains
Third-party scripts: no inline event handlers
Third-party scripts: tracking scripts respect privacy
Third-party scripts: no deprecated or unmaintained libraries
